note: January at Anamalai: 14–26°C, cool dry forest. Doable — but early-morning safaris fog-bound, bamboo rafting runs on reduced flow after dry spell.
January at Athirapally: 20–32°C, post-monsoon flow thinning. Falls still run but nothing like monsoon volume — shoulder month for the main draw.
January at Coonoor: 8–20°C, cold tea-garden mornings with heavy frost. Toy train runs, but expect layers and short outdoor windows.
January at Kodaikanal: 6–18°C at 2133m, cold with frequent fog on Coaker's Walk. Lake walks workable — just bundle up and pick mid-day.
January at Kotagiri: 9–21°C, Nilgiri cold with misty mornings. Quiet tea-estate walks work if you can handle the chill and late-rising sun.
January at Mudumalai: 14–27°C, dry season but dense morning fog dulls early safaris. Doable — afternoons clear, sightings improve mid-day.
January at Ooty: 5–18°C at 2240m, frost on lawns and fog until mid-morning. Toy train and gardens work — shoulder month if you prefer dry sun.
January at Yercaud: 12–24°C, cold Eastern Ghats mornings. Coffee estates quiet — workable, just accept thick fog until late morning.
January at Ziro: 4–16°C at 1780m, cold and empty. Apatani villages open but trails stiff with frost — shoulder month, not music-festival season.
February at Athirapally: 22–33°C, falls at lowest flow before pre-monsoon. Forest drive still scenic — shoulder month, not the showpiece volume.
February at Bhimashankar: 13–28°C, dry Sahyadri. Jyotirlinga darshan easy, giant squirrels active — treks doable, just nothing like the monsoon green.
February at Coonoor: 9–22°C, cold tea-country mornings easing by month-end. Workable — fog lifts slower than March, outdoor time still compressed.
February at Courtallam: 22–33°C, flow dropping before the Tamil monsoon returns. Herbal baths still run — shoulder month, not peak cascade.
February at Khandala: 14–29°C, dry ghats with clear Duke's Nose views. Doable — but valleys are brown, nothing like the monsoon cascades.
February at Kodaikanal: 7–20°C, still cold at 2133m with night frost. Lake and Coaker's Walk work — just warmer by mid-February than January.
February at Kotagiri: 10–23°C, cold mornings softening. Tea estates quiet and tribal villages accessible — shoulder month, transition to dry March.
February at Ooty: 6–20°C, cold with late-clearing fog. Botanical garden and toy train run — workable, just bring layers and plan late starts.
February at Yercaud: 13–26°C, easing out of winter. Coffee-season tail, quiet weekends — doable, warmer by month-end, still no monsoon drama.
May at Alleppey: 26–34°C, humid pre-monsoon. Houseboats run but cabins stifling midday — shoulder month, book AC boats and early cruises.
May at Bekal: 27–33°C, humid Arabian coast. Fort walks work early or late — doable, just nothing like the post-monsoon clarity.
May at Beypore: 27–33°C, humid and hot at the Uru yards. Shipyard tours doable in early hours — shoulder month, low-season quiet.
May at Cherai: 27–33°C, humid with rough pre-monsoon swell. Dolphin sightings reduced, swim flags often up — workable, not peak beach time.
May at Eravikulam: 12–24°C at 2000m, park closed for Tahr calving through early-May. Opens mid-month — shoulder, narrow window before monsoon.
May at Guruvayur: 26–34°C, humid and busy with summer pilgrim surge. Darshan queues long — doable if you start pre-dawn, not a quiet month.
May at Idukki: 20–32°C, hazy cardamom hills pre-monsoon. Dam reservoir low, sanctuary dry — workable, just past the viewing sweet spot.
May at Kadmat: 28–32°C, warm seas but choppy pre-monsoon. Diving windows narrow, permits tight — shoulder, book early and expect cancellations.
May at Kannur: 27–34°C, humid Malabar coast. Theyyam season tails off early-May — drive-in beach doable, ritual calendar thin.
May at Kochi: 27–33°C, humid Fort Kochi with occasional pre-monsoon showers. Walking doable early or late — Biennale off, shoulder for the city.
May at Kollam: 27–34°C, humid Ashtamudi shore. 8-hour cruise to Alleppey runs but sticky — workable, not the clear post-monsoon view.
May at Kovalam: 27–33°C, humid with rough pre-monsoon swell. Ayurveda centres quieter and cheaper — shoulder, swim flags often restrict sea.
May at Kozhikode: 27–34°C, humid Malabar. Beaches breezy, biryani trail always on — doable, just low-season with pre-monsoon haze.
May at Kumarakom: 26–34°C, humid Vembanad. Siberian migrants gone — birding thin, houseboats discounted. Shoulder month for the lake.
May at Kumbalangi: 27–33°C, humid village air. Crab farms and fishing-net walks doable early-morning — shoulder, backwaters at lowest clarity.
May at Kuttanad: 27–34°C, humid with paddies between harvest and sowing. Below-sea-level farms visible — workable, just not the green-saturated season.
May at Marari: 27–33°C, humid fishing coast with rough swell. Village walks work — doable, just past the calm-sea window of February–March.
May at Meghamalai: 18–28°C at 1500m, cloud forest still cool before monsoon closures. Estate bookings tight — shoulder window, narrow access.
May at Munnar: 16–28°C at 1600m, warmer haze over tea estates. Workable — just peak crowds from plains, and clarity drops before June rains.
May at Nelliyampathy: 18–29°C, plantations dry and hazy pre-monsoon. Birding thinner — shoulder, quiet but not the green-drenched view.
May at Ponmudi: 20–30°C, hazy hairpin drive from Trivandrum. Butterfly counts down — doable as a cool day-break, not prime habitat month.
May at Poovar: 27–33°C, humid estuary. Boat-access resort runs but pre-monsoon swell rough — shoulder, river side better than sea side.
May at Thekkady: 18–31°C, dry Periyar with reduced lake level. Elephants still congregate — workable, spice plantations hot by midday.
May at Trivandrum: 27–34°C, humid capital. Padmanabhaswamy and museums doable if you stay indoors midday — shoulder, pre-monsoon sticky.
May at Vagamon: 18–28°C, meadows brown before rains. Paragliding weather borderline — shoulder, not the green-pine season photos promise.
May at Valparai: 18–30°C, 40 hairpins still driveable but cloud forest hazy. Macaque sightings steady — shoulder, cooler than plains.
May at Varkala: 27–33°C, humid cliff with rough pre-monsoon sea. Yoga shalas quieter, rates lower — shoulder, swimming often flagged.
May at Wayanad: 20–32°C, dry spice country pre-monsoon. Caves and trails open but hazy — workable, far from the Ghats at full green.
June at Coonoor: 14–22°C, southwest monsoon thickening. Tea gardens lush but toy train cancellations common — workable between showers.
June at Hogenakkal: 24–33°C, Kaveri rising with early flows. Coracle rides run but suspended on high-water days — shoulder, oil-massage trail fine.
June at Kodaikanal: 12–22°C, monsoon-grey at 2133m. Coaker's Walk fogged most days — doable, lake walks work between spells.
June at Kotagiri: 14–23°C, monsoon in the Nilgiris. Tea-estate walks run but slippery — shoulder, viewpoints fog-locked much of the day.
June at Ooty: 12–22°C, southwest monsoon peaking. Toy train runs subject to landslides — workable, plan indoor buffers and bring rain gear.
June at Pillalamarri: 25–33°C, early Telangana monsoon. Aerial roots drip, grounds muddy — workable half-day, Sufi tombs slippery underfoot.
June at Ramappa: 25–32°C, monsoon clouds framing the UNESCO temple. Moat fills, paths muddy — doable, dramatic photos if rain times cooperate.
June at Saputara: 20–29°C, Gujarat's only ghat station turning monsoon. Tribal villages wet — shoulder, fog heavy, outdoor time short.
June at Yercaud: 16–26°C, early Eastern-Ghat monsoon. Coffee estates green again but lake viewpoints fog-bound — workable, expect short windows.
July at Ambaji: 24–32°C, humid with southwest rain. Shakti Peeth darshan on — workable, the hilltop climb wet and slippery in peak monsoon.
July at Dhauli: 26–32°C, Odisha monsoon thick. Peace pagoda in mist is moving — doable half-day, edicts hard to read when wet.
July at Diamond Triangle: 26–32°C, monsoon greens the Buddhist ruins. Doable — paths muddy across Lalitgiri-Ratnagiri-Udayagiri, short windows between cells.
July at Halebidu: 20–28°C, soft Karnataka monsoon. Hoysaleshwara panels look striking wet — workable, pace for short rain bursts.
July at Junagadh: 25–32°C, humid Saurashtra monsoon. Girnar steps slick, Ashokan edicts accessible — shoulder, full climb risky on rain days.
July at Kolanupaka: 25–32°C, Telangana monsoon greening the Jain temple grounds. Quiet visit — workable, moat full, paths muddy.
July at Konark: 26–32°C, Odisha monsoon with dramatic skies behind the Sun Temple. Doable — expect short closures when storms roll in.
July at Medak: 23–30°C, Telangana monsoon. 500 cathedral steps become treacherous — workable for church interior, fort climb risky.
July at Undavalli: 25–32°C, monsoon along the Krishna. Rock-cut caves stay dry but approach muddy — shoulder, greenest view of the riverbank.
August at Ambaji: 24–31°C, peak Gujarat monsoon. Shakti Peeth temple busy for Shravan — doable, hilltop walks slick underfoot.
August at Bidar: 22–29°C, north-Karnataka monsoon. Fort and Bidriware workshops doable — shoulder, open-plan mausoleums wet and muddy.
August at Champaner-Pavagadh: 23–30°C, peak monsoon. UNESCO complex green and quiet — doable, Pavagadh climb slippery, ropeway helps.
August at Chitradurga: 21–28°C, soft Deccan monsoon. Seven walls green again — workable, boulder scrambles slick, take the lower loop.
August at Daman: 25–30°C, west-coast monsoon thick. Portuguese forts stand but beaches rough — shoulder, indoor heritage stronger than coast.
August at Diamond Triangle: 26–31°C, Odisha monsoon lush. Buddhist ruins atmospheric in mist — doable, expect muddy trails between sites.
August at Halebidu: 20–27°C, Karnataka monsoon softening. Hoysala sculptures glisten — workable, one of the quietest months at the temple.
August at Junagadh: 24–30°C, peak Saurashtra monsoon. Buddhist caves and Nawabi core accessible — shoulder, Girnar climb dangerous in rain.
August at Kolanupaka: 24–30°C, quiet Telangana monsoon. Jade Mahaveer darshan contemplative — doable, offbeat temple village you'll have to yourself.
August at Konark: 26–31°C, Odisha monsoon with moody skies. Sun Temple panels stand out wet — workable, windows between showers suffice.
August at Medak: 22–29°C, softer monsoon. Cathedral interior quietly powerful — shoulder, fort's 500 steps remain slick, pick one site.
August at Raghurajpur: 26–31°C, Odisha monsoon. Pattachitra artists prep festival inventory — doable, village lanes muddy, homes dry and welcoming.
August at Shravanabelagola: 20–27°C, Karnataka monsoon. Bahubali hill climb wet and barefoot — shoulder, take the slow step-by-step pace.
August at Srirangapatna: 21–28°C, Kaveri swollen around Tipu's island. Temple and fort doable — workable, some river-edge paths flooded.
August at Statue of Unity: 25–31°C, Narmada valley monsoon. Viewing gallery on but visibility low — shoulder, dam views stronger than skyline.
October at Anamalai: 20–29°C, late southwest monsoon trailing. Safaris and bamboo rafting run — doable, expect muddy tracks and occasional closures.
October at Auroville: 24–31°C, northeast monsoon starting. Matrimandir visits need advance slots — shoulder, organic farms wet underfoot.
October at Bekal: 24–31°C, southwest monsoon retreating. Fort walks doable between showers — workable, seas still rough for beach time.
October at Chennai: 25–32°C, northeast monsoon building. Marina breezy, heritage walks work — doable, expect sudden showers and flooded lanes.
October at Cherai: 24–31°C, post-monsoon clearing. Dolphin sightings returning, seas still choppy — shoulder, swim flags often up.
October at Chettinad: 24–32°C, early NE monsoon. Mansion walks and cuisine trail run — doable, expect dust-to-wet transition and short showers.
October at Chidambaram: 25–31°C, northeast monsoon onset. Nataraja darshan unaffected — workable, temple corridors wet, town floods possible.
October at Chinnar: 18–28°C, Kerala's dry sanctuary still wet from SW monsoon. Doable — trails muddy, Thoovanam Falls at peak flow.
October at Coimbatore: 22–31°C, pre-NE monsoon. Adiyogi visit and Nilgiri gateway runs work — shoulder, humid with afternoon showers building.
October at Dhanushkodi: 25–31°C, NE monsoon season for this east-coast tip. Jeep access cut on heavy-rain days — shoulder, check before the drive.
October at Girnar: 22–31°C, Saurashtra drying after monsoon. 10,000-step climb doable but steps still slick — workable, ropeway safer if rain lingers.
October at Kadmat: 26–31°C, transition window. Diving and kayaking returning — shoulder, permits slow and seas still settling from monsoon.
October at Kanchipuram: 25–32°C, early NE monsoon. Temple trail runs, silk weavers open — doable, short heavy showers interrupt walking.
October at Kanyakumari: 25–30°C, NE monsoon onset. Three-seas viewpoint often cloud-covered — shoulder, sunrise-sunset windows hit-or-miss.
October at Kovalam: 25–31°C, SW monsoon retreating. Beach reopening, Ayurveda centres ramping — shoulder, seas still rough early in month.
October at Kumbakonam: 25–32°C, NE monsoon onset. Airavatesvara and temple trail run — doable, expect afternoon showers in the Cauvery delta.
October at Kutch: 22–34°C, post-monsoon transition. Craft villages open, Aina Mahal doable — shoulder, the white Rann doesn't dry out until November.
October at Madurai: 25–33°C, early NE monsoon. Meenakshi midnight ritual runs as always — doable, heat eases but afternoon showers common.
October at Mahabalipuram: 25–31°C, NE monsoon starting on the Coromandel. Rock-cut heritage doable — shoulder, shore walks between showers.
October at Marari: 25–31°C, SW monsoon retreating. Fishing village quiet, seas still rough — shoulder, low-season rates, flags often up.
October at Mudumalai: 18–28°C, wet biosphere post-SW monsoon. Safaris run but trails muddy — workable, elephant sightings steady, tiger low.
October at Palitana: 22–33°C, post-monsoon Saurashtra. 863 Jain temples and climb doable — shoulder, start pre-dawn to beat heat and afternoon showers.
October at Point Calimere: 24–31°C, early NE monsoon. Flamingos arriving, blackbuck visible — shoulder, wetland paths flooded in heavy showers.
October at Pondicherry: 25–31°C, NE monsoon onset. French Quarter cafes and Auroville run — doable, expect flooded lanes on heavy-rain days.
October at Rameswaram: 25–31°C, NE monsoon season. Char Dham darshan unaffected — shoulder, Pamban drive and temple tank exposed to rain.
October at Silent Valley: 18–27°C, rainforest still wet post-SW monsoon. Permit treks run — workable, leech-heavy, canopy sightings limited.
October at Srirangam: 25–31°C, NE monsoon onset near Trichy. Island temple darshan runs — doable, 21 gopurams photo-friendly between showers.
October at Thanjavur: 25–32°C, early NE monsoon in the delta. Brihadeeswara noon-shadow trick runs — shoulder, museum and fort indoor buffers help.
October at Tharangambadi: 25–31°C, NE monsoon on the Coromandel. Fort Dansborg visible — shoulder, beach rough, church tour indoor-safe.
October at Thrissur: 24–31°C, post-SW monsoon. Temple complex and town walks run — workable, Pooram is April so culture is baseline, not peak.
October at Trichy: 25–32°C, early NE monsoon. Rock Fort climb doable but steps slick — shoulder, expect afternoon downpours across the city.
October at Varkala: 24–31°C, SW monsoon retreating. Cliff shacks reopening, yoga restarting — shoulder, sea still rough, flags up most days.
October at Vedanthangal: 24–31°C, NE monsoon filling the tank. Early nesters arriving — shoulder, peak numbers come November–January.
October at Velavadar: 22–34°C, grassland post-monsoon. Blackbuck visible, harriers arriving — shoulder, haze reduces morning visibility until late-October.
November at Anamalai: 18–27°C, NE-monsoon still soaking the reserve. Safaris on but trails muddy — shoulder, sightings steady, not the dry-clear window.
November at Mudumalai: 16–27°C, tail of the NE monsoon in the biosphere. Safaris run — workable, morning fog and damp trails until late month.
November at Nagarhole: 16–27°C, late monsoon still wet in the Kabini zone. Safaris on — shoulder, sightings build but early-month mud slows drives.
December at Anamalai: 15–26°C, cool dry-season window opens. Safaris good but early-December fog heavy — shoulder, bamboo rafting on reduced flow.
December at Athirapally: 22–32°C, flow thinning after monsoon. Forest drive scenic — workable, waterfall nothing like the June volume showpiece.
December at Hogenakkal: 22–31°C, Kaveri flow settling. Coracle rides run — shoulder, falls narrower than monsoon, oil-massage tradition always on.
December at Mudumalai: 14–26°C, dry season setting in. Safaris good — doable, thick morning fog on the Nilgiri edge compresses prime hours.
December at Nagarhole: 14–27°C, post-monsoon cooling. Kabini safaris run — workable, water levels still high, sightings concentrate late afternoon.
